findings.title: Batch delete access logging now copies every object key
findings.impact: DeleteAllCopy previously logged ObjectKeysToAbbrStr(req.object_keys()), which only needed the first key and count. The new ObjectKeysRef(req.object_keys()) path stores a PROTO_REF, and ObjectKeyState::FillObjectKey materializes it by reserving protoRef->size() and emplacing every key into a std::vector before formatting. With the default access sample rate of 1.0, every sampled-in batch delete pays O(number of keys) heap allocations and string copies on the worker request path, even though the log output is bounded/truncated.
findings.fix: Keep this call on a summary path: add/use a protobuf-compatible ObjectKeysSummaryRef or ObjectKeyProvider that formats only the first key and count, and change the PROTO_REF formatter to stream directly from the repeated field instead of copying it into a vector.
